ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper proposes a distinct new mechanism ("sound combiners") that symbolically captures the loop-carried dependence in SGD via a combiner matrix Ma→b = ∏(I−αXTi·Xi) and uses Johnson–Lindenstrauss random projection to produce "probabilistically sound" combiners that preserve sequential SGD semantics in expectation. This combination of symbolic combiners, projecting M−I to control variance, and using the projection during a reduction to exactly (in expectation) reproduce sequential behavior is a non-trivial technical contribution that is clearly differentiated from prior asynchronous/averaging schemes (HOGWILD!, ALLREDUCE, parameter servers) discussed in the paper. The approach is novel within its stated scope (SGD updates linear in the model) and would be of clear interest to experts, though it is limited to that class of learners.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (Intro → method → experiments → conclusion) and states its main contribution (SYMSGD, sound/probabilistic combiners) early in the Introduction. The technical notation is mostly consistent and key objects are defined explicitly (e.g. combiner Ma→b, Eq.2 and Lemma A.1) and algorithms are supported by clear pseudocode. Presentation weaknesses include several typographical/formating errors and repetitions (e.g. “udpate”, “infact”, “expclitly”, and duplicated paragraphs in Appendix A.5), occasional minor index/notation awkwardness (product notation in Lemma A.1), and many figures/tables are referred to but not present in the provided text, which hampers readability.

SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper’s core derivations are technically sound: Lemma A.1 correctly derives the combiner Ma→b = ∏(I − α X_i^T X_i) for linear-regression SGD and the update MA = (I − α X^T X)M is implemented correctly in the pseudo-code; Lemma 3.1 (IE[A A^T]=I) and the unbiasedness of using M A A^T are also correct. The variance bounds, the rank claim for N = M−I, and other detailed concentration/variance analyses are stated but deferred to the technical report (so the main text lacks full proofs), which is a minor completeness gap. Experimental methodology is reasonable (tuned strong sequential baseline, multiple implementations, parameter search, 5-run averages) though no formal statistical tests are reported. Overall the technical argumentation supports the main claims, with only modest omissions of detailed proofs and variance-convergence analysis in the main paper.
